hero.name: Kelsey
hero.tagline: Mental Health Advocate | Stigma Fighter | Patient-Centric

narrative.title: Why psychiatry, why AbbVie
narrative.body:
I spent the first part of my career in institutional finance, most recently at Goldman Sachs. The real skill there wasn't the numbers — it was earning the trust of time-strapped clients, reading what each one needed, and knowing dense, legal-jargon contracts well enough to solve real problems with them. In 2023, I left to become a full-time parent.

In 2025, I watched a stranger save someone's life during a medical emergency. That moment pushed me to earn my EMT license. During my ER clinicals, I saw two kinds of psychiatric patients: those with no physical injury who were clearly suffering, and those whose physical emergencies traced back to an untreated psychiatric condition. I had long cared about mental health, but seeing those patients in every ER pushed me to be part of the solution.

I've always been achievement-oriented and tenacious, so when a friend told me my personality was built for pharmaceutical sales, it clicked. I looked for a product I could genuinely believe in, and that search led me to AbbVie Psychiatry. My reasons are personal. People I love have struggled with mental illness, and I know the helplessness of watching it. I've seen what the right medicine and advocacy can do, and the cost when neither arrives: loneliness, homelessness, suicide. As a CASA volunteer for abused and neglected children, I saw how few resources reach the people who need them most. That is why I want to be part of this space.

AbbVie has a reputation for patient-centric care, and Vraylar is the centerpiece of its psychiatry portfolio. Once I looked into it, I understood why. It works differently than most other antipsychotics: a partial agonist with unusually strong dopamine binding, which means it can lift someone up or level them out depending on what they need. It's approved for bipolar I depression and as an add-on for major depressive disorder, for patients who've tried an SSRI, SNRI, or mood stabilizer and still aren't better. That's a huge population. A large share of people treated for depression actually have undiagnosed bipolar depression underneath it, and this is one of the only options built for exactly that gap. What I like just as much is that AbbVie shows up for mental health outside of any branded conversation too, awareness events, screening tools, real investment in breaking the stigma before someone ever gets to a prescription. And the pipeline behind it keeps growing. Bretisilocin and ABBV-932 show AbbVie's commitment to innovation and to real solutions for mental health. Reading that AbbVie's neuroscience business is now its fastest-growing, multi-billion-dollar therapeutic area makes me excited for where this team is headed.

I also know this market. I lived in Prosper the past few years, and McKinney and the DFW suburbs are growing faster than the psychiatric provider base can keep up with, so every prescriber here is carrying a heavier load. My approach is simple: know my product inside out, build real trusting relationships, listen for what each provider needs, and be the solution. I recently spoke with Casey Yeager, a rep on the neuroscience team, who spoke with real pride about this company and its leadership, and that conversation only made me more excited to bring that same energy to McKinney.

startStories.title: Sales Performance & Brand KPIs
startStories.teaser: "Deliver sales performance, brand KPIs, financial targets, marketing objectives, etc. to meet or exceed on those objectives."
startStories.body:
At Vivint, I wasn't in the field, I was setting the targets the field reps had to hit. I built the executive forecasting models and diagnosed the performance gaps that became the strategic goals for the team, then presented a business case to senior leadership that secured a $2M+ revenue expansion opportunity. That's the metric-setting side of a brand KPI: understanding where the number should come from before anyone chases it.

At Goldman Sachs, hitting the number meant something more literal. I completed 10 to 20 complex transactions a week with zero-defect accuracy, in a 70+ hour workweek with no room for error. That's the discipline of consistently meeting hard output targets under real pressure, the same discipline it takes to hit a quota month after month.

startStories.title: Pre-Call Planning & Post-Call Evaluation
startStories.teaser: "Create pre-call plan objectives and execute post-call evaluation to continuously improve sales performance... consistently gain logical and reasonable calls to action to close on every sales call."
startStories.body:
As a volunteer representative across Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota, I built this process from scratch. Before every visit, I wrote a specific pre-call plan objective. After every visit, I ran a post-call evaluation, tracked through a CRM pipeline, capturing the objections and concerns I heard so I could handle them better next time. That discipline turned an underperforming territory into one of the strongest territories in the region.

At Goldman Sachs, the same instinct showed up differently. When a senior client pushed back on a contract term, I found the misread myself, got internal leadership aligned, and closed the objection out before it became a lost account. Prepare for the objection, then close with a clear next step, works the same whether the stakes are a contract or a prescribing decision.

startStories.title: Trusted Relationships & Win-Win Agreements
startStories.teaser: "Proactively and continuously aspire to serve customer needs, customer expectations and challenges to build trusted customer relationships and to achieve win-win agreements."
startStories.body:
The clearest example of this is a community leader I worked with as a volunteer representative, the gatekeeper to an entire network of prospects. He hadn't worked with a single missionary in years, and everyone before me said not to bother. Instead of pushing him directly, I built a relationship with his wife over weekly lunches until I learned he missed playing soccer with a group he'd lost touch with. I organized a recurring soccer event and told him all he had to do was show up. He did, and over time he went from ignoring us completely to mobilizing his own network on our behalf, and eventually inviting us to celebrate the Fourth of July with his family, something no missionary before me had ever been invited to. Resistance is usually just information about what someone actually values.

At Goldman Sachs, I managed 20+ institutional relationships with senior decision makers who had every option available to them and no obligation to stay loyal. Relationship-first account management, reading what each one needed and delivering it, is what kept them.

startStories.title: Call Planning & Territory Coverage
startStories.teaser: "Develop and execute a call plan that achieves set call metrics and optimizes coverage and frequency to key customers to maximize access and sales opportunities."
startStories.body:
Across a 3-state mission territory, I built a repeatable cadence of visits and referrals instead of relying on cold outreach, the same coverage-and-frequency thinking a call plan requires.

At 1-800 Contacts, I built a nationwide marketing heat map across 42,000+ U.S. ZIP codes to identify exactly where spend and coverage should be concentrated for maximum reach, the analytical backbone of deciding who gets called on, and how often.

startStories.title: Market & Clinical Intelligence
startStories.teaser: "Continuously build understanding on customer needs and expectations, territory market landscape, competitors, market segments/dynamics... to optimize brand strategy and its execution."
startStories.body:
At Vivint, I diagnosed territory-level performance gaps and built executive forecasting models in technical SQL, Snowflake, and Python to understand exactly where the market was moving and why.

At 1-800 Contacts, I didn't just build the market intelligence, I shared it. After building the ZIP code targeting framework, I personally trained the Analytic Team on how to use it, the same alignment this role asks for between field insight and the broader team.

And on the clinical side, my EMT training in the psychiatric section of the ER, plus coursework in psychiatric emergency response and pharmacology fundamentals, is where my disease-state and product fluency for this therapeutic area starts.

startStories.title: Identifying & Developing Speakers and Advocates
startStories.teaser: "Differentiate AbbVie's value proposition with health providers assigned and identify, develop, and maintain disease state experts and speakers/advocates to maximize brand performance."
startStories.body:
During my 18 months as a Spanish-speaking volunteer representative, each area had an appointed community leader who was essentially the gateway between local members and our work. Without his engagement, we were cut off from the exact network our prospects needed to join. Mine hadn't worked with a single missionary in years, and everyone who came before me said not to even bother.

So instead of pushing him directly, I built a relationship with his wife first: weekly lunches, genuine friendship, listening for anything that told me who he actually was. She eventually mentioned he missed playing soccer with a group, so I organized a community soccer event and told him all he had to do was show up. He came, and we made it recurring. Over time he went from ignoring us completely to making referrals and mobilizing his entire network, and he eventually invited us to celebrate the Fourth of July with his whole family, something every missionary before me had called unimaginable. That's the same motion this bullet describes: find the person whose voice already carries weight, develop them on their own terms, and let their credibility open doors a cold pitch never could.

At Goldman Sachs, I served as the face of the firm's brand to senior financial decision-makers, executing white-glove standards specifically to differentiate Goldman's value proposition from competitors, the brand-differentiation half of this requirement.

skills.items: Spanish — professional working proficiency · Licensed EMT (NREMT) · BLS Certified (AHA) · FEMA Certified · B.S. Communications, Minor in Finance — Brigham Young University

experience.title: Licensed Emergency Medical Technician
experience.body:
Top 5% of cohort, delivering hands-on clinical support across the emergency room, trauma bay, ambulance, and in-transit care.

I provided direct patient care within the emergency department's dedicated psychiatric section and completed training in psychiatric emergency response and pharmacology fundamentals, the exact behavioral health fluency and patient-centered focus this role calls for. Assisting an ER physician during a high-acuity emergency tracheotomy, and supporting emergency air transport intake from rooftop helipad arrival through trauma bay admission, taught me to stay calm and execute under real pressure while working alongside physicians, nurses, EMTs, firefighters, and trauma teams, the same matrix-environment collaboration a specialty rep needs across a prescriber's care team.

experience.title: Global Markets Analyst — Client Relationships & Syndicated Loan Operations
experience.body:
Managed 20+ institutional relationships across multi-million dollar syndicated loan portfolios, completing 10 to 20 complex transactions weekly with zero-defect accuracy in a 70+ hour workweek.

The real work wasn't the transactions, it was making a busy client's day easier: reading what they actually needed and solving it before they had to ask twice. When one pushed back on a contract term, I found the misread myself and got internal leadership aligned to keep the account, the same objection handling and calls to action a specialty rep needs to close every sales call. Getting fluent in dense, legal-jargon-filled contracts well enough to solve real problems with them is the same skill it takes to get fluent in dense clinical and scientific language fast: mechanism of action, payer language, competitive data, and making it land for the person across the desk.

experience.title: Business Operations Analyst
experience.body:
Diagnosed territory-level performance gaps and built executive forecasting models in technical SQL, Snowflake, and Python to translate complex data into clear, board-aligned growth strategy.

Presenting a C-suite business case that secured a $2M+ revenue expansion opportunity is the same market intelligence and business acumen this role needs from a rep who can read a territory's competitive landscape and turn that insight into brand strategy that actually gets executed in the field, not just planned on paper.

experience.title: Financial Analyst Intern
experience.body:
Built a nationwide marketing performance heat map across 42,000+ U.S. ZIP codes to identify underperforming spend, market segments, and high-potential growth territories, then trained the Analytic Team on the framework I built.

I taught myself SQL from scratch to build it, the same way I'd teach myself the technical and scientific language of a therapeutic area: unfamiliar to fluent, fast, and useful enough to share with the team the way AbbVie expects reps to feed field intelligence back to their brand and sales manager.

experience.title: Executive Director (Volunteer) — BYU Chapter
experience.body:
Presided over a 5-person leadership team overseeing 100+ volunteer advocates supporting court-involved children in a high-stakes judicial environment.

Many of the children I advocated for were navigating serious mental health needs, which gave me an early, first-hand understanding of what's at stake in psychiatric care. Building trusted relationships with judges, attorneys, families, and multidisciplinary stakeholders through emotionally intelligent communication is the same patient-centered, matrix-environment stakeholder management a psychiatry rep needs to operate credibly across a prescriber's full care team.

experience.title: Full-Time Volunteer Representative — Nebraska, Iowa & South Dakota
experience.body:
Grew membership across a 3-state territory by identifying and developing influential community advocates who referred and influenced others, reducing reliance on cold outreach.

I went in with classroom Spanish and came out fluent, using it every day for 18 months with hundreds of families and individuals across Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota, proof I can pick up a new language, technical or otherwise, and actually put it to work in the field. Creating pre-call plan objectives and executing post-call evaluations, tracked through a CRM pipeline, is the same call planning discipline this role requires to optimize coverage and frequency to key customers, and onboarding and mentoring a new representative while consistently hitting daily and weekly targets is the coachability and leadership AbbVie looks for in a rep who can build referral-driven access from scratch.
